title: [luxray] Battle Guide
summary: A physical [electric] pivot defined by [intimidate] on entry, [wild-charge] and [crunch] damage, and [volt-switch] escape routes despite only base 70 Speed.
strengths: One of the only physical [electric] attackers in the format with native [intimidate]
[wild-charge], [crunch], and [ice-fang] cover almost every physical target in two hits
[volt-switch] escapes lose matchups and keeps momentum flowing
weaknesses: 4x effective pressure from [ground] priority checks and base 70 Speed puts it behind the curve
Overshadowed by faster physical [electric] alternatives in the meta
[wild-charge] recoil makes long games expensive on its HP bar

recommendedMoves.reason: Main STAB damage option, taking recoil for maximum hit power
punishes Ghosts and Psychics and catches [electric]-resist pivots
is the required coverage against Dragon, [ground], and Flying walls
preserves momentum in bad matchups and enables pivot chains

guarantees one turn of survival into scarfers or [ground] priority

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[luxray] earns its slot by being one of the only physical [electric] attackers in the format that carries [intimidate] on entry. The physical [electric] niche is thin, and almost every other option lacks a way to shave the opponent's Attack for free. Base 120 Atk and 70 Speed are awkward on their own, but [intimidate] plus [volt-switch] pivot play turn those numbers into real value.

## Strengths
STAB [wild-charge] from 120 Atk with a Jolly nature puts [luxray] at the top of the physical [electric] damage chart even without a damage-boosting item. [crunch] breaks Ghost and Psychic walls while also catching Grass and [electric]-resist pivots that would otherwise stall it out. The real tool is [ice-fang], which turns the usual Dragon/[ground]/Flying answer list into a clean 4x coverage chart. Dropping in mid-turn with [intimidate] lets [luxray] blunt an incoming physical hit and then [volt-switch] out into the correct counter on your side.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[luxray] is 2x weak to [ground] and has a shallow 70 base Speed, so any Scarf user or priority physical threat forces it into trades it does not want. Physical [ground] walls such as Hippowdon-style tanks eat everything [luxray] has outside of [ice-fang] and hit back for huge damage. [wild-charge] recoil is the silent killer in long matches — stacked recoil plus stealth-rock chip will kill [luxray] before a second setup window ever opens.

## Recommended Moveset
[wild-charge] is the locked main STAB, but treat it like a kill-confirm button rather than a spam move — recoil is the biggest HP drain in its set. [crunch] is the secondary STAB-adjacent slot that lets [luxray] actually threaten Ghost and Psychic types and punish Grass pivots that would normally come in for free on an [electric]. [ice-fang] covers Dragon, [ground], and Flying all at once and is non-negotiable. The fourth slot is [volt-switch], which is what actually makes [luxray] a pivot rather than a worse version of other physical Electrics. A superpower swap-in is the best alternative if the team lacks a reliable answer to Normal, Rock, and Steel walls.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sitrus-berry] is the default item — it offsets one [wild-charge] recoil cycle and pushes [luxray] over the HP threshold it needs to get a second attack off. [focus-sash] is the aggressive alternative that guarantees survival against one priority or Choice Scarf user and buys a free [volt-switch] turn. The ability is locked to [intimidate]; it is the entire reason to run [luxray]. For Tera, [electric] deepens [wild-charge] damage, while Tera [ground] removes the 2x [ground] weakness entirely and turns [luxray] into a surprise defensive check.

## Teammates & Synergy
[luxray] needs a Grass or Flying pivot to absorb the [ground] attacks that would otherwise force it out — pair it with a slow U-turn partner so that [volt-switch] and U-turn form a forced-momentum loop. A special cleaner in the back capitalizes on the physical cushion [intimidate] creates. A rocks-remover is nice but not mandatory, since [luxray] is not expected to switch in and out more than twice per match.

## Play Patterns
On turn one, prioritize [intimidate] entry over offense — drop the opponent's Attack before committing to a damage move. Press [wild-charge] only on kill-confirmed ranges, since repeated recoil is what usually ends [luxray]'s game. Once HP dips below a third, [volt-switch] out and let the pivot chain do the cleanup work.
